name: Equal Parts and the Meaning of a Fraction
whatIsIt: A fraction tells us about equal parts of a whole. When we cut something into equal pieces, a fraction names how many of those pieces we are talking about. In 3/4, the bottom number (4) says the whole is cut into 4 equal parts, and the top number (3) says we are talking about 3 of them.
whyNeeded: Numbers like 1, 2, 3 can count whole things, but life is full of pieces! Half a roti, quarter of an hour, part of a chocolate bar. Without fractions we cannot say how much of something we have when it is not a whole. Fractions give names to pieces.
realLifeUses: Sharing food fairly: cutting a dosa, pizza, or chocolate bar so everyone gets an equal share
Time: 'half an hour' is 1/2 of 60 minutes; 'quarter past' is 1/4 of the way around the clock
Money: 50 paise is 1/2 of one rupee
Cricket: a bowler has bowled 1/2 of the overs; the team has finished 3/4 of the match
Measuring: half a cup of milk, quarter kg of sugar in Amma's recipe

vocabulary.meaning: The complete thing before cutting — the full roti, the full bar, the full hour.
Pieces that are all exactly the same size.
A number that names equal parts of a whole, written like 3/4.
The TOP number — how many parts we are talking about.
The BOTTOM number — how many equal parts the whole is cut into. D for Down!

story.title: The Wise Myna and the Diwali Barfi
story.text: It is Diwali. Three little sparrows find one square barfi left on the sweet-shop tray. 'Mine!' 'No, mine!' they chirp, tugging at it. Chatur the wise old myna flies down. 'Friends, share it — but FAIRLY. For fair sharing, every piece must be EQUAL, exactly the same size.' She helps them cut the barfi into 3 equal pieces, one for each sparrow. 'Each of you gets one part out of three equal parts — that is one-third, written 1/3.' The sparrows stop squabbling and share happily.
story.extractedProblem: One Diwali barfi is cut into 3 equal pieces and shared by 3 sparrows. What fraction of the barfi does each sparrow get?
story.answerInStory: Each sparrow gets 1/3 (one-third) of the barfi — one equal piece out of three.

visual.caption: Same whole, different equal parts. Tap to shade parts and read the fraction: shaded parts on top, total equal parts on the bottom.

teachingGallery.title: A fraction = shaded parts out of equal parts
teachingGallery.note: The whole is cut into EQUAL parts. Top = shaded, bottom = total parts.

standardMethod.summary: To write a fraction: count the total equal parts (bottom number), count the parts you are talking about (top number).
steps: Check: are ALL the parts equal? If not, it is not a fraction yet.
Count the total number of equal parts. Write it as the BOTTOM number (denominator).
Count the parts you want (shaded, eaten, chosen). Write it as the TOP number (numerator).
Read it: 3/4 is 'three-fourths' — 3 parts out of 4 equal parts.

alternateMethods.name: Paper folding (concrete method)
alternateMethods.whenToUse: When learning a new fraction for the first time, or when a picture is not enough.
steps: Take a sheet of paper — this is the whole.
Fold it once: 2 equal parts. Each part is 1/2.
Fold again: 4 equal parts. Each part is 1/4.
Color some parts and say the fraction: colored parts over total parts.
alternateMethods.example: Fold a paper twice, color 3 of the 4 parts: the colored part is 3/4.

alternateMethods.name: Sharing method (division connection)
alternateMethods.whenToUse: When something is shared equally among friends.
steps: Count the friends sharing — that is the bottom number.
One fair share for one friend is 1 over that number.
alternateMethods.example: 1 dosa shared equally by 4 friends: each gets 1/4 of the dosa.

workedExamples.problem: A chocolate bar is divided into 6 equal pieces. Meena eats 2 pieces. What fraction of the bar did Meena eat?
steps: Are the parts equal? Yes — 6 equal pieces.
Total equal parts = 6, so the bottom number is 6.
Meena ate 2 parts, so the top number is 2.
Meena ate 2/6 of the chocolate bar.

workedExamples.problem: A circle is cut into 4 parts, but the parts are NOT the same size. Rahul shades 1 part and says 'I shaded 1/4.' Is he right?
steps: First rule of fractions: the parts must be EQUAL.
These 4 parts are not the same size.
So the shaded part is NOT 1/4 of the circle.
Rahul must cut the circle into 4 EQUAL parts before he can say 1/4.
workedExamples.answer: No — the parts are not equal, so it is not 1/4.

trickPractice.intro: Count the shaded parts on top, the total equal parts on the bottom — that's the fraction.

questions.q: A shape has 4 equal parts, 3 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 3/4.

questions.q: A shape has 2 equal parts, 1 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 1/2.

questions.q: A shape has 3 equal parts, 2 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 2/3.

questions.q: A shape has 8 equal parts, 5 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 5/8.

questions.q: A shape has 5 equal parts, 2 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 2/5.

questions.q: A shape has 6 equal parts, 3 are shaded. Write the fraction.
hintLadder: Bottom number = total EQUAL parts.
Top number = how many are shaded.
Fraction = 3/6.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counting parts that are not equal in size and still writing a fraction (calling 1 big piece out of 3 unequal pieces '1/3').
commonMistakes.fix: Always check EQUAL first. If parts are different sizes, redraw or refold until every part matches. No equal parts, no fraction!

commonMistakes.mistake: Writing the total parts on top and the chosen parts on the bottom (writing 4/3 instead of 3/4).
commonMistakes.fix: Remember: Denominator is Down — the total equal parts go DOWN at the bottom. The parts you talk about go on top.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counting the unshaded parts instead of the shaded parts for the top number.
commonMistakes.fix: Read the question again: which parts is it asking about? Point and count ONLY those parts for the top number.

easy.q: A roti is cut into 2 equal parts. You eat 1 part. What fraction did you eat?
hintLadder: How many equal parts is the roti cut into? That number goes on the bottom.
You ate 1 part. Where does the 1 go — top or bottom?
1 part eaten, 2 equal parts in total: one number on top of the other.

options.label: A square cut into 4 same-size small squares
A square cut into 1 big piece and 2 small pieces

easy.answer: A square cut into 4 same-size small squares
hintLadder: Equal means every piece is exactly the same size.
Look at each piece — could you swap any two pieces and see no difference?
In one picture the pieces match perfectly. That one shows equal parts.

easy.q: A strip is cut into 3 equal parts and 2 parts are shaded. Write the fraction for the shaded parts.
hintLadder: Count ALL the equal parts first — that is the bottom number.
Now count only the SHADED parts — that is the top number.
Shaded 2, total 3. Write top over bottom.

medium.q: A chocolate bar has 8 equal pieces. Ravi eats 3 pieces. What fraction of the bar did Ravi eat?
hintLadder: Total equal pieces = 8. Where does 8 go?
Ravi ate 3 pieces. Where does 3 go?
Parts eaten on top, total equal parts below: 3 over 8.

medium.q: A cake is cut into 5 equal slices. 4 slices are eaten. What fraction of the cake is LEFT?
hintLadder: Careful — the question asks what is LEFT, not what was eaten.
5 slices in total, 4 eaten. How many slices remain?
1 slice left out of 5 equal slices. Write that as a fraction.
medium.explain: 4 eaten means 1 slice remains: 1/5 of the cake is left.

medium.q: One hour has 60 minutes. Half an hour is 1 part when the hour is cut into how many equal parts? Write 'half an hour' as a fraction of one hour.
hintLadder: Half means the whole is cut into 2 equal parts.
Half an hour is 1 of those 2 equal parts.
1 part out of 2 equal parts — write it as a fraction.

challenge.q: Anu cut her square barfi into 4 pieces: one BIG piece and three small pieces. She gave the big piece to Ravi and says 'Ravi got 1/4.' What is wrong?

options.label: The parts are not equal, so 1/4 is not correct
Nothing is wrong, 1 piece out of 4 is always 1/4

challenge.answer: The parts are not equal, so 1/4 is not correct
hintLadder: What is the FIRST rule before writing any fraction?
Is the big piece the same size as each small piece?
1/4 only works when all 4 parts are exactly equal. Are they?
challenge.explain: Fractions need equal parts. Ravi's big piece is MORE than 1/4 of the barfi.

challenge.q: A dosa is shared equally among 4 friends. Two friends give their full shares to Fraction Fox. Along with his own share, what fraction of the dosa does Fraction Fox have now?
hintLadder: Each friend's share is 1 part out of 4 equal parts — 1/4 each.
Fox has his own share plus shares from 2 friends. Count the parts: 1 + 1 + 1.
Fox has 3 parts out of 4 equal parts.

questions.q: In the fraction 3/8, what does the 8 tell us?

options.label: The whole is cut into 8 equal parts

questions.answer: The whole is cut into 8 equal parts
hintLadder: 8 is at the bottom — the denominator.
D for Down, D for 'divided into'.
The bottom number counts the total equal parts.

questions.q: A strip has 5 equal parts; 4 are shaded. Write the fraction for the shaded part.
hintLadder: Count total equal parts for the bottom.
Count shaded parts for the top.
4 shaded out of 5 equal parts.

questions.q: Which of these can be called 1/3?

options.label: 1 piece out of 3 EQUAL pieces of a bar

questions.answer: 1 piece out of 3 EQUAL pieces of a bar
hintLadder: The first rule of fractions is about the size of parts.
Can unequal pieces make a fraction?
Only equal parts can be named by a fraction.

questions.q: A pizza has 6 equal slices. 5 slices are eaten. What fraction of the pizza is left?
hintLadder: The question asks about what is LEFT.
6 slices total, 5 eaten — how many remain?
1 slice left out of 6 equal slices.

questions.q: One rupee = 100 paise. 50 paise is what fraction of one rupee? (Think: 100 cut into two equal parts of 50.)
hintLadder: How many 50-paise parts make one full rupee?
Two equal parts of 50 make 100.
50 paise is 1 part out of 2 equal parts of a rupee.

teachBackPrompt: Fraction Fox forgot what a fraction is! Explain to Fox in your own words: what must be true about the parts, what the top number means, and what the bottom number means. Use a roti, chocolate, or anything you like as your example.

revisionCard.summary: A fraction names EQUAL parts of a whole. Bottom number (denominator) = total equal parts (D for Down/Divided). Top number (numerator) = parts we talk about. 3/4 = 3 parts out of 4 equal parts. No equal parts, no fraction!

gameMission.title: The Fair-Share Feast
gameMission.brief: Guests are arriving at the Fraction Kitchen! Cut each dish into equal parts, serve the right fraction to each guest, and spot the sneaky unequal cuts before Ravi serves them.

realLifeProject: At snack time today, take one roti or chapati. Fold-and-cut it into 4 equal parts with a parent. Give parts to family members and say each person's share as a fraction ('Appa got 1/4... now Appa has 2/4!'). Draw what you did in your math notebook.
